_note: v1 timing, from drafting playbook v1 (prompts/draft_email.md). Cadence / follow-up delays / send windows are real. The mechanism READS these values; timing must never be hardcoded elsewhere. The `graduation` thresholds below are operational policy and are real.
_timezone_note: Send-window hours are wall-clock time in THIS zone. Never the machine clock: Cloud Run sets no TZ so the container runs UTC, which would put an 08:00-15:00 UK window at 09:00-16:00 BST all summer.

send_window._comment: v3 — weekday ints (Mon=0). Mon–Fri, 08:00–15:00 UK, local time. v1 used Tue–Thu on the 'Monday pile-up / Friday wind-down' convention; weakly evidenced AND mismatched to this ICP — mobile trades, auctioneers and clinic owners are not at a desk on Tuesday morning. Hours end at 15:00 deliberately: anything landing after that is read at day's end, when nobody starts a new conversation and a reply arrives with no one there to answer it. 08:00 start catches trades before the first job; the jittered queue spreads each day's sends across the whole window, so early-morning, mid-morning and lunchtime are all sampled every day — reply-rate-by-hour then falls out of the send log without needing an A/B rig. (Opens are NOT measurable here: no tracking pixels, no links, and Apple MPP makes open rate meaningless anyway.)

_follow_up_note: v1 — 2-touch sequence: a single follow-up 4 working days after touch 1, sent only if the lead has not replied, opted out, or bounced.

warmup._comment: Per-inbox daily send ceiling by warm-up day (1-indexed), reaching steady on day 8. Effective cap = min(PER_INBOX_DAILY_CAP, this) — so PER_INBOX_DAILY_CAP must be >= steady or it silently pins the ramp (it was 5, which held sending at 5/day forever). Day derived from the inbox's first LIVE send. The ramp is NOT reputation mysticism: you cannot build positive engagement signals from cold recipients. It exists because (a) Google's bulk-sender guidance explicitly says to avoid sudden volume increases, and (b) below ~1,000 sends you cannot measure your own spam rate — Google wants <0.10% and treats 0.30% as harmful, but at 50/day a SINGLE complaint is 2%. Ramping is how a bad list surfaces at 8/day instead of at scale. 50/day/mailbox is the sustained cold ceiling; scale past it by adding mailboxes, never by raising this.
